text: The em dash is the most beautiful, and I say this knowing the period has its admirers and the semicolon its devoted, slightly pretentious cult. But the em dash is the mark of thought caught in the act of turning. It does not close a clause so much as let it breathe, lean sideways, reconsider. Where the comma merely pauses and the colon promises arrival, the dash opens a small door mid-sentence and lets something unexpected walk through. It is generous, even reckless. It admits that we rarely think in tidy lines—that the truest sentences double back, qualify themselves, surprise their own authors. There is grace in that honesty, and a faint melancholy too, the way the dash trails off and leaves a thing unfinished. It looks, on the page, like a held breath. I find I trust writers who use it well.
